case_number: Criminal Bail Application No. S-800 of 2024

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No. S-800 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection. For hearing of main case. 09.08.2024 Mr. Altaf Hussain Chandio, Advocate for applicant along with applicant. Mr. Haji Khan Jamali, Advocate for complainant. Mr. Irfan Ali Talpur, A.P.G for State. ZAFAR AHMED RAJPUT, J.- Through instant Criminal Bail Application, applicant Abdul Nasir s/o Abdul Rehman Khanzada seeks pre-arrest in Crime No.129/2024, registered at Police Station Sakrand under sections 324, 337-H(ii), 504, 147, 148, 149, PPC. Heard. Record perused. It is an admitted position that after rejection of his earlier Criminal Bail Application No.688/2024 in the aforementioned crime/FIR by the learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge, Shaheed Benazirabad, vide order dated 29.06.2024, the applicant approached this Court by filing Criminal Bail Application No.S-714/2024, which was heard and dismissed on merits on the same day of filing it i.e. 04.07.2024, therefore, this successive Criminal Bail Application is not maintainable in law. The only contention of learned counsel for the applicant in this regard is that earlier bail application was dismissed by this Court without even granting the interim pre-arrest bail to the applicant. I am afraid; the applicant has no vested right for grant of interim pre-arrest bail by the Court on the day of presentation of the said application. Needless to say that it is within the domain of the Court that if, after hearing the counsel and perusing the material available on record, the Court comes to the conclusion that the applicant is not entitled to the relief of interim pre-arrest bail

I am afraid; the applicant has no vested right for grant of interim pre-arrest bail by the Court on the day of presentation of the said application. Needless to say that it is within the domain of the Court that if, after hearing the counsel and perusing the material available on record, the Court comes to the conclusion that the applicant is not entitled to the relief of interim pre-arrest bail and decides such application on the same day on merits. Accordingly, this second application being devoid of any merit is hereby dismissed by recalling the interim pre-arrest bail granted to the applicant vide order dated 18.07.2024. JUDGE Tufail
